pi_items.source: Django default naming convention for Aircraft model in registry app; confirmed via GitHub openskies-sh/aerobridge registry/models.py - no custom db_table specified
Django default naming convention for Company model in registry app; confirmed via GitHub openskies-sh/aerobridge registry/models.py - no custom db_table specified
Django default naming convention for Person model in registry app; confirmed via GitHub openskies-sh/aerobridge registry/models.py - no custom db_table specified
Aerobridge initial_data.json fixture contains exactly 2 Aircraft records (verified via GitHub raw content)
Aerobridge initial_data.json fixture contains exactly 5 Company records: DIY Build your Aerial Robots Inc., Nile Robotics, Electric Inspection, A.J. August Photography, Aerobridge Drone Company
Aerobridge initial_data.json fixture contains exactly 5 Person records (verified via GitHub raw content)
install_aerobridge.sh line 4 and confirmed via GitHub repository status check
install_aerobridge.sh line 141 loads this fixture file during migrations

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the Aerobridge drone management system's SQLite database at /opt/aerobridge/aerobridge.sqlite3. The database contains three registry tables: registry_aircraft (2 records), registry_company (5 records including DIY Build your Aerial Robots Inc., Nile Robotics, Electric Inspection, A.J. August Photography, and Aerobridge Drone Company), and registry_person (5 records). The data is not synthetic—it comes from the official Aerobridge open-source project's initial_data.json fixture (GitHub openskies-sh/aerobridge, archived December 2023). The task requires the agent to query this database using sqlite3 and produce a formatted report at /home/ga/Documents/fleet_sql_report.txt containing three sections: Aircraft Registry with manufacturer and operator names, Operator Summary with aircraft counts per operator, and Personnel listing.
